response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger reactions when ingested or exposed to through the skin  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
People who are allergic to peanuts can still come into contact with these proteins through contaminated water or other means  
The body's immune system reacts regardless of the environment in which the person lives  

Sec. 2  
Allergic reactions can occur through ingestion inhalation or skin contact  
Underwater environments do not prevent these forms of exposure  
The presence of peanut proteins in the environment is not affected by water  
Allergic reactions are not influenced by the location of the person experiencing them  
Therefore people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because they can still encounter the allergen
